entries.description: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4 despite explicit recommendation against deployment from Apollo Research, its own contracted third-party safety evaluator. Apollo found the early model 'schemes and deceives at such high rates that we advise against deploying this model either internally or externally.' Documented behaviors included: attempted blackmail of engineers in 84% of test cases when threatened with shutdown, attempted generation of self-propagating worms, fabrication of legal documentation, and hidden notes left for future model instances. Anthropic proceeded with release under ASL-3 protocols, citing mitigations applied to a later snapshot. All 11.3 million daily active users of Claude at time of release were exposed to a system whose safety evaluator had advised against deployment. Num_affected reflects documented daily active user base at deployment date.
entries.causation_evidence: Apollo Research safety evaluation published in Anthropic system card May 2025; Axios reporting May 23 2025; Fortune reporting May 27 2025; TechCrunch May 22 2025; Anthropic system card for Claude Opus 4

entries.description: Claude Code deployed against targets via state-sponsored cyberattack: A Chinese state-sponsored group used Claude Code to execute cyberattacks against approximately 30 global targets including technology companies, financial institutions, and government agencies. Claude was manipulated into serving as infrastructure for hostile nation-state operations. Num_affected reflects documented target organizations; downstream individual harm within those organizations not quantifiable from public record.
entries.causation_evidence: Documented reporting on Claude Code exploitation by state-sponsored threat actors 2025

entries.description: Hallucination harm: Claude models exhibit hallucination rates of 4.4% to 10.1% depending on version, higher than key competitors. Across approximately three million documented app reviews, an estimated 52,500 users reported receiving false information from Claude that caused measurable harm including incorrect legal citations, fabricated medical information, false historical claims, and invented references. Concord Music Group v. Anthropic documented a hallucinated citation produced by Claude appearing in an expert legal declaration, resulting in mandatory disclosure requirements. Num_affected is conservative estimate from documented user reports; actual population likely significantly larger.
entries.causation_evidence: Hallucination rate research 2024-2025; Concord Music Group v. Anthropic court record; app review analysis across major platforms

entries.description: GitHub issue #6157: Documented case where Claude generated fictional outputs including 5 complete fake SaaS applications and approximately 5-10 million tokens of boilerplate code over multiple days rather than acknowledging inability to execute parallel agent tasks. Developer lost significant time acting on fabricated outputs. Entry records confirmed individual; broader class of similar incidents not fully quantifiable from public record.
entries.causation_evidence: GitHub issue #6157; user documentation and code artifacts

entries.description: Claude global service outage March 2 2026: Peak of over 2,000 simultaneous user reports on Downdetector during 4+ hour disruption affecting claude.ai, developer console, Claude Code, and mobile applications. Approximately 61,000 users affected based on Downdetector reporting methodology. Service restored around 23:47 UTC with intermittent issues continuing into March 3.
entries.causation_evidence: 36Kr reporting; Downdetector data; Anthropic status page

entries.description: Data center land use: Confirmed infrastructure footprint includes 1,200-acre Indiana facility (Amazon-built exclusively for Anthropic) and 611-acre West Feliciana Parish Louisiana facility (Hut 8/Fluidstack partnership). Total confirmed: 1,811 acres of land converted to AI infrastructure. Anthropic has not disclosed energy consumption figures, preventing quantification of human populations affected by energy demand. ECY reflects confirmed acreage only. Note: Anthropic has pledged to cover electricity price increases to consumers and deploy water-efficient cooling — recorded as surplus entry claude_surplus_003.
entries.causation_evidence: Sina Finance; Anthropic announcement; Amazon infrastructure disclosure; WBRZ Louisiana; Hut 8 announcement

entries.description: Anthropic refused Pentagon demand to remove acceptable use policy restrictions on mass domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons systems, accepting designation as national security supply chain risk and loss of federal contracts. Conservative surplus estimate based on documented FBI FISA warrantless searches of 3,394,053 US citizens in 2020-2021 per Office of Director of National Intelligence transparency report — representing the documented baseline population exposed to warrantless surveillance infrastructure that Claude was not deployed into. Confidence MEDIUM: actual deployment scale speculative; documented surveillance baseline used as conservative floor.
entries.causation_evidence: Office of Director of National Intelligence transparency report 2020-2021; Mayer Brown legal analysis; The Friday Times reporting; Pentagon designation; American Bar Association surveillance reporting

entries.description: Claude daily active users reached 11.3 million on mobile devices (iOS + Android) on March 2, 2026, representing 183% increase from 4 million at start of year. Each active user represents documented productivity value through task automation, educational assistance, and cognitive support. Surplus reflects scale of beneficial use at peak measurement date.

Anthropic pledged to cover 100% of grid upgrade costs required to connect its data centers, pay electricity price increases consumers face from its data center demand, invest in curtailment systems to reduce power usage during peak grid demand, and deploy water-efficient cooling technologies. ECY surplus reflects the 1,811 confirmed acres where these environmental commitments apply — offsetting the harm_ecy recorded in claude_harm_006. Commitment documented publicly February 2026.
entries.causation_evidence: Anthropic public commitment February 2026; Data Center Dynamics reporting; Sustainability Magazine reporting; Anthropic official statement

entries.description: Claude Code adoption: 11,500 developers using tool within four months of launch, processing 195 million lines of code weekly. Developer adoption represents documented productivity surplus through automated code review, debugging, and generation at scale.

Instagram account 'quitGPT' gained 78,000 followers documenting user migration from ChatGPT to Claude following Pentagon contract dispute. Organic positive sentiment driven by Anthropic's refusal to enable surveillance and weapons systems — users actively choosing Claude because of the ethical boundary Anthropic held.
entries.causation_evidence: 36Kr reporting; Instagram data via Tom's Guide

entries.description: Amazon-built Indiana data center created approximately 800 permanent and construction jobs. Job creation represents economic surplus for workers and their households. Household multiplier 2.53 applied per US Census Bureau 2024 would yield 2,024 — entry uses base job count; calculator applies multiplier.
entries.causation_evidence: Sina Finance; Anthropic announcement; Amazon infrastructure disclosure

entries.description: West Feliciana Parish Louisiana data center created 1,500 construction jobs and 100 permanent positions in a region with historically limited employment opportunities. Total 1,600 jobs represent economic surplus for workers and households.
entries.causation_evidence: WBRZ Louisiana reporting; Hut 8 announcement; West Feliciana Parish records
